free_text: Improved 192,194,195,196Pt(n,gamma) and
192Ir(n,gamma) astrophysical reaction rates
Experiment performed at the Oak Ridge
Electron Linear Accelerator (ORELA)
For neutron capture measurements a pair of
C6D6 detectors were used and the pulse-height-weighting
technique was employed. A 10B filter was
used to remove overlap neutrons from preceding beam
bursts and a Pb filter was used to reduce gamma
flash effects.
Neutron energy was determined by time of flight
Neutron capture measurements were made on flight
path 7 at a source-to-sample distance of 40.12 m
The ORELA was operated at a pulse rate of 525
Hz, a pulse width of 8 ns, and a power of 7-8 kW.
Cross-section normalization was made via the saturated
resonance technique using the 4.9-eV resonance
in the 197Au(n,gamma) reaction.
Corrections for multiple scattering, self shielding,
and isotopic impurities were made.
Compiled by S.H.
